title: hieracium caesium subsp. fastigiellum
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. fastigiellum, also known as the tall hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the dandelion family that grows to be around 1-2 meters tall. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions and tinctures, which can provide health benefits including reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make a poultice, which can be applied topically to reduce swelling and promote wound healing.
